records.ety: [L. permutatio: cf. F. permutation. See Permute.]

defs: The act of permuting; exchange of the thing for another; mutual transference; interchange.
quotes: The violent convulsions and permutations that have been made in property.

defs: The arrangement of any determinate number of things, as units, objects, letters, etc., in all possible orders, one after the other; -- called also alternation. Cf. Combination, n., 4.
Any one of such possible arrangements.
